channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Voters flood town halls with fears of Social Security cuts, putting heat on GOP over Musk and DOGE
item.description: HOLLAND, Mich. (AP) — One after another, callers on a telephone town hall with U.S. Rep. Bill Huizenga pressed the Michigan Republican about possible cuts to Social Security.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Large crowds converge in Belgrade for protest rally against Serbia's president and government
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Tens of thousands of people descended on Belgrade on Saturday as part of a mass rally seen as a culmination of months-long protests against Serbia's populist President Aleksandar Vucic and his government.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC and DUSAN STOJANOVIC, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: United Airlines gate agent punched in face, passenger arrested
item.description: Police said an airline passenger faces charges after he punched a United Airlines gate agent Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Brian Farrell, NewsNation

item.title: Massive shark headed down Florida coast ahead of busy travel season
item.description: Florida researchers have identified a record-breaking great white shark as it is tracked down Florida's coastline ahead of spring break.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Worst storms of 2025 will be outside Tornado Alley, severe weather forecast predicts
item.description: Tornadoes can (and have) hit every U.S. state, but the area nicknamed Tornado Alley is probably where your mind goes when you think of dangerous twisters. This year, that's not be the region of highest concern, according to a severe outlook released this month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Missing American student: Dominican lawyer puts drowning theory back in play; same beach claimed 4 in January
item.description: A Dominican lawyer believes the possibility that missing Pitt student Sudiksha Konanki drowned is more plausible than some experts have indicated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Extreme weather expected Saturday gets rarely used 'high risk' designation
item.description: A dynamic storm system threatening to spawn powerful tornadoes and hail as big as baseballs has earned a relatively rare designation from forecasters.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Martin, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: After Columbia arrests, international college students fall silent
item.description: In the span of a week, a hush has descended on higher education in the United States. International students and faculty have watched the growing crackdown on pro-Palestinian protesters at Columbia University with apprehension.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Critics warn staff cuts at federal agencies overseeing US dams could put public safety at risk
item.description: Experts are warning that Trump administration workforce cuts at federal agencies overseeing U.S. dams are threatening their ability to provide reliable electricity, supply water to farmers and protect communities from floods.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 4 arrested in shooting death of 7-year-old Florida boy
item.description: "This is just the first round of arrests we will make in bringing Breon’s family the justice they deserve," the sheriff's office said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, Fox 5

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge denies temporary relief in lawsuits challenging Guantánamo migrant detentions
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to grant temporary injunctive relief to plaintiffs in two lawsuits challenging the Trump administration’s efforts to detain migrants at Guantánamo Bay. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Helicopters permanently restricted on route near Washington airport where 67 died in midair crash
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Helicopters will be permanently restricted from flying near Washington, D.C.'s airport on the same route where a passenger jet and an Army helicopter collided in midair, killing 67 people, the Federal Aviation Administration said Friday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN SEEWER and TARA COPP, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boy, 13, critically injured while subway surfing in New York
item.description: He was taken to NYC Health + Hospitals/Elmhurst, police said. An investigation into the incident is ongoing, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, NewsNation

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, NewsNation

item.title: Judge formally sentences Adnan Syed to time served in 'Serial' podcast case
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — A Maryland judge formally sentenced Adnan Syed on Friday to the time he's already served in prison, appearing to finally bring to a close a long-running case with numerous legal twists and turns that received worldwide attention from the true-crime podcast “Serial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma brought by the Biden administration’s Department of Justice, which challenged the states' immigration laws making it a crime for someone to be in the state if they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: DHS revokes visa of one Columbia student, arrests another pro-Palestinian protester
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security (DHS) announced Friday it has revoked the visa of one Columbia University student and arrested another person involved in the pro-Palestinian protests, escalating its crackdown on those engaged in demonstrations over the conflict in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, WFLA, NBC

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, Fox 5

item.title: Key witness in American college student's disappearance tells police how they met, what happened on beach
item.description: Key witness in missing American college student Sudiksha Konanki's disappearance in the Dominican Republic says they were hit by a big wave before she vanished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Arrest made after crash kills at least 5 people, including infant, in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Air taxis flying over Austin could transform future commutes
item.description: "You can come and train in VR and in less than an hour you can solo fly vertical lift aircraft," Chasen said.
item.dc:creator: Eric Henrikson, NewsNation

item.title: ICE Boston arrests Guatemalan national charged with child rape who was released back into community by court
item.description: Guatemalan national, Stivenson Omar Perez-Ajtzalan, 19, was arrested on Jan. 30 for aggravated rape of a child 10 years younger than him, according to ICE Boston.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Luigi Mangione's defense fund raises over $722K
item.description: "People are raising money not for his innocence but because they're in support of allegedly what he did.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Vargas, NewsNation

item.title: Federal judge rules Trump must reinstate many fired federal employees
item.description: A federal judge ruled Thursday that the Trump administration must reinstate probationary government employees fired unlawfully at several agencies, lambasting the Justice Department at a hearing for a “sham” gambit that enabled a key official to avoid testifying in the case. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Europe with 200 percent tariff on wine, Champagne
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday threatened to put a hefty 200 percent tariff on wine and Champagne coming from Europe if the European Union (EU) does not remove a tariff on whisky.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Connecticut woman arrested after stepson allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of kidnapping a man and holding him captive for over 20 years was arrested Wednesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Education Department layoffs gut its civil rights office, leaving discrimination cases in limbo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Education Department’s civil rights branch is losing nearly half its staff in the Trump administration’s layoffs, effectively gutting an office that already faced a backlog of thousands of complaints from students and families across the nation.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA ends program that helped schools serve food from local farmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Agriculture Department is ending two pandemic-era programs that provided more than $1 billion for schools and food banks to purchase food from local farmers and producers.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 4-year-old calls 911 to say 'bad mom' ate his ice cream and needed to go to jail
item.description: We've all been there. You go to the freezer and discover your ice cream has been eaten, and not by you.
item.dc:creator: Laura Morrison, WFLA, NBC

item.title: T-Mobile customers to get payments up to $25K next month after data breach: Here's who qualifies
item.description: The $350 million T-Mobile agreed to pay in a 2022 class action settlement will finally start going out to customers next month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: EPA signals it could narrow Clean Water Act protections
item.description: The Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) has signaled that it could narrow which set of waters receive protections under the Clean Water Act.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: LA woman reunited with cat presumed lost in devastating Palisades Fire
item.description: A California woman who lost her home in the devastating firestorm that engulfed the Palisades experienced a tearful reunion over the weekend with her beloved cat, lost in the charred remains of the neighborhood for some two months.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WGN 9
